name: Marijampole County (Lithuania)

colors.symbolism: the outer field of Lithuanian county flags and the field of the Marijampole arms

details of the central coat of arms

description: A blue field with the coat of arms of Marijampole County in the centre, set within an inner panel framed by the standard Lithuanian-county blue border.
history: Like the flags of all ten Lithuanian counties, Marijampole County's flag follows the uniform template introduced after the country's redivision into apskritys (counties) in 1994: a blue field surrounding the county's heraldic device.

features.symbolism: blue outer border in the Lithuanian county pattern, surrounding the county's heraldic field

features.role: coat of arms of Marijampole County
features.symbolism: the heraldic device of Marijampole County, recalling the religious and Suvalkija heritage of the region
